summary: Iteration 72: Tested HttpOnly cookie authentication migration and new features. All core authentication flows work correctly - login sets HttpOnly cookie AND returns token in JSON, /api/me works with both cookie and Bearer token auth, logout clears cookie. GDPR export endpoint works for customers. Integration status and email provider endpoints work for admins. Partner code is displayed correctly in Profile page.

issues: React hydration warnings for span inside select/option - minor console warning but doesn't affect functionality

critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: HttpOnly cookie implementation is correct with proper security flags (httponly=True, secure from env, samesite from env)
security.py: Token extraction correctly prioritizes Authorization header over cookie for backward compatibility
AuthContext.tsx: Properly stores token in localStorage AND relies on HttpOnly cookie - supports both auth methods
api.ts: withCredentials=true correctly enables cookie-based authentication
GDPR endpoints properly restrict access to customers only (admins get 403)

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15 core tests passed, 5 skipped due to rate limiting)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (Login, Admin panel, Customer portal, Profile page all working)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created - used existing test users
context_for_next_testing_agent: HttpOnly cookie auth is fully working. Key points: 1) Login endpoints set aa_access_token cookie AND return token in JSON body for backward compat. 2) /api/me and other protected endpoints accept both cookie auth and Bearer token auth. 3) Logout clears the HttpOnly cookie. 4) GDPR export/delete endpoints are customer-only (403 for admins). 5) Integration status endpoints require admin role.

tenant_b_admin_access.login: PASS (verified via curl after rate limit cleared)

mocked_apis_note: Zoho Mail/CRM API calls are MOCKED (credentials can be saved but OAuth flow not complete). Email sending is MOCKED unless provider is validated and activated.
